visionSystem.title: Fabric Quality control using High Speed camera system
description: AI-powered vision platform delivering customizable, scalable solutions for quality control, traceability, and process optimization across industries.
features: Superhuman inspection speed with Micro-level accuracy
High-resolution fabric imagery for advanced defect detection
Integrated with optimization engine for improved yield
Synchronized with cutting machines for efficient roll-making
Digital traceability and reduced reliance on manual inspection
Quick return on investment through enhanced operational efficiency
Working on Denim, Dyed & Greige Fabrics. Knits & Prints Coming Soon.

cameraIndustries.title: Camera Inspection in Other Industries
description: EVS (Edraak Vision System) provides reliable camera-based inspection for a wide range of industrial production lines. High-resolution cameras continuously scan products and surfaces to detect defects, damages, or inconsistencies in real time during production.
Used in pharma, food, electronics, and automotive, it quickly detects defects, reduces waste, and ensures consistent product quality.

subtabs.name: Greige Fabric
subtabs.content: EVS inspects greige (raw/unfinished) fabric straight from the loom, detecting common defects like knots, slubs, holes, broken yarns, thick/thin places, and uneven weave in real time. This prevents faulty greige from moving to dyeing, cuts down on rework, improves yield, and keeps weaving consistent.

subtabs.name: Dyed
subtabs.content: EVS checks dyed fabrics for issues that appear after coloring, such as uneven dyeing (patchy or cloudy shades), stains, dye marks, streaks, and weave faults visible post-dye. Running at full production speed, it flags problems instantly for marking or removal, helping maintain uniform color quality and avoid buyer rejections.

subtabs.name: Denim
subtabs.content: EVS specializes in denim inspection, identifying streaks, holes, broken yarns, slubs, neps, barre marks, and finishing defects on the fly. In busy denim lines, it increases first-quality output, shortens manual checks, lowers rejection rates, and helps meet strict buyer standards.

subtabs.name: On-Loom
subtabs.content: EVS on-loom inspection monitors fabric quality directly during weaving to detect defects at the earliest stage. Real-time alerts help operators react immediately, reducing downstream losses, rework, and machine downtime.

subtabs.name: Apparel Inspection
subtabs.content: EVS apparel inspection checks stitched garments for visual defects, stitching issues, shade mismatches, and labeling errors before dispatch. It supports consistent final quality and faster quality assurance decisions.
